item.title: Southwest revises its overweight passenger rules. Here's what's changing.
item.description: Southwest is walking back some recent changes in its policies for passengers who require a second seat.

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass congressional map designed to pick up GOP seat
item.description: The Louisiana Legislature passed a new congressional map that would leave the state with only one of its two majority-Black districts.

item.title: Pam Bondi testifies behind closed doors in House committee's Epstein probe
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi testified before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.

item.title: Trump discussing Iran deal in Situation Room
item.description: President Trump assembled members of his national security team to discuss the latest proposal on Iran. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson report.

item.title: Trump says he's "meeting now" to make decision on Iran deal
item.description: Trump said Friday that Iran must agree to never have a nuclear weapon and to reopen the Strait or Hormuz immediately, without tolls.

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks DOJ work on $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Justice Department announced the $1.7 billion fund as part of a settlement of a civil lawsuit President Trump brought against the IRS.

item.title: FAA chief reacts to Mullin's suggestion to halt international flights to Newark over ICE protests
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration Administrator Bryan Bedford is reacting to Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin's suggestion to halt international flights to Newark, New Jersey, amid tense ICE protests. Lindsey Reiser reports.

item.title: ICE agent accused of shooting man in north Minneapolis arrested in Texas
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent accused of shooting a man in the leg in north Minneapolis and then lying about the attack​ was arrested in Texas Friday morning, according to the Hennepin County Attorney's Office.

item.title: Pam Bondi testifying on Epstein files as survivors demand accountability
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying privately before members of the House Oversight Committee on the botched Justice Department rollout of the Epstein files. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: White House has yet to release results of Trump's latest physical
item.description: The White House has yet to release a summary the results of President Trump's latest physical exam.

item.title: 1 miner rescued after over a week trapped in flooded cave
item.description: The lead rescue diver told "CBS Mornings" earlier Friday that teaching the trapped miners how to scuba dive might be the only way to free them.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine is bracing for big attacks from Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that his country is bracing for "big attacks" from Russia on Friday or Saturday night. "Our people have to be very, very careful, cautious, and children, and they have to use bomb shelters...nobody knows 100%, but there is a high percent," Zelenskyy said in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: Many artists drop out of Freedom 250 concerts shortly after lineup announced
item.description: Many artists announced for the Freedom 250 concert series in Washington, D.C., this summer, say they won't be performing.

item.title: Multiple artists announced for America's 250th anniversary concert drop out
item.description: Multiple artists announced for America's 250th anniversary concert are no longer performing. CBS News' Lindsey Reiser reports.

item.title: How ChatGPT warped reality for these users: "Why would the AI lie to me?"
item.description: AI-fueled delusions can happen when chatbots respond to grandiose, paranoid or imaginary ideas with affirmation or encouragement.

item.title: Trump calls Situation Room meeting to discuss latest on Iran deal
item.description: President Trump said on social media that he's meeting with members of his national security team to discuss the latest round of negotiations with Iran. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and Eleanor Watson report.

item.title: Can inherited debt impact your Social Security benefits?
item.description: Inheriting debt isn't common, but when it happens, it's important to understand the potential consequences.

item.title: Wyclef Jean releases first of huge 7-album music project
item.description: Musician Wyclef Jean is on a journey to release 7 albums as part of a single project titled "Quantum Leap." Jean joined CBS News with more details.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 31)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Former chef pleads guilty to supplying lethal substances for suicides
item.description: Police in Canada and around the world have been investigating more than 100 suicides linked to Kenneth Law.

item.title: Wyclef Jean explains why he's releasing 7 albums in a year and inspiration behind his music
item.description: Grammy Award-winning artist Wyclef Jean released a new single, "Mr. October," from his new album "Clef Notes," which comes out June 26. The album is the first installment of this seven-album project "Quantum Leap." Jean joins to discuss why he plans to release seven albums in one year and the story behind his new single.

item.title: 5 dead, dozens injured as bus plows into 6 vehicles in Virginia
item.description: The five deaths came in vehicles that were struck by the bus when it did not slow down for traffic.

item.title: Can Buy Now, Pay Later debt impact Social Security recipients?
item.description: This common payment tool can impact retirees' benefits and finances, sometimes in ways they don't expect.

item.title: 3 CD account moves savers should make before the June Fed meeting
item.description: A Federal Reserve meeting is set for mid-June. Here are three moves potential CD account holders should make now.

item.title: Could vultures help fight invasive pythons? Study documents rare attack
item.description: Researchers say vultures feeding on Burmese python eggs in the Everglades marks the first documented case of native birds preying on a python nest in Florida.

item.title: Shrey Parikh of California wins 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee in spell-off
item.description: Shrey Parikh of Rancho Cucamonga, California, emerged victorious Thursday in the 98th annual Scripps National Spelling Bee.

item.title: Economist who predicted past three World Cup winners says this team will win 2026 contest
item.description: German economist Joachim Klement, who has predicted the past three World Cup winners, joins CBS News' Ramy Inocencio about his 2026 pick.

item.title: Jill Biden says husband "was slowing down" but wasn't in cognitive decline
item.description: Jill Biden said she never saw signs her husband, former President Joe Biden, was in cognitive decline, in an interview for "CBS Sunday Morning" airing May 31.

item.title: Ex-chef who supplied poison for suicides to avoid murder charges
item.description: Kenneth Law is a former chef accused of running a number of online forums that offered predominantly young, distressed people advice on how to end their lives.

item.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
item.description: The brutal murders of four teenage girls has haunted Austin, Texas, for 30 years. Could new information lead to a killer? "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty has reported on the case from the beginning and has the latest on the search for answers. | CASE UPDATE: In September 2025, the Austin Police Department identified Robert Eugene Brashers, a serial killer and rapist, as the suspect in the Texas yogurt shop murders. Brashers, who is deceased, was tied to the murders through DNA testing. On Feb. 19, 2026, a judge declared innocent the four men who were wrongfully accused of the murders — formally exonerating them. In May 2026, the City of Austin agreed to pay a settlement of $35 million to be split among the men.
